goal: Validate whether Codex step 3 multi-agent protocol has an executable foundation for the Research Pipeline using provided local artifacts.
scope: Local documentation only (no web); focus on the requested multi-agent protocol files.
summary: Mapped the Research Pipeline’s acceptance criteria and structured-output rules so Retriever, Verifier, and Synthesizer each have a minimal executable task: Retriever catalogs constraints from the architecture guide and README, Verifier checks the shared base contract’s prompt/output rules and fallback guidance, and Synthesizer merges the resulting state_delta into a coherent verification report for the Planner.

facts: The global enhancement guide defines the Research Pipeline topology (Planner, Retriever, Verifier, Synthesizer) and the requirement for structured JSON output and fallback suggestions.
The multi-agent README lists the step 3 acceptance checks, emphasizing full JSON returns, state_delta merging, and fallback behavior.
The shared base contract enforces the layered prompt stack, output rules, and data safety policies that every Retriever/Verifier/Synthesizer role must follow.
The example input specifies the precise goal, scope, and artifacts that anchor this validation effort.
preferences: Lean solely on the supplied local artifacts without dropping to external sources.
Keep each subagent’s responsibilities narrowly focused so their deliverables remain traceable to the documented protocol.
decisions: Retriever will extract and summarize acceptance criteria plus artifact references from the architecture guide and README.
Verifier will verify compliance with the base contract’s prompt and JSON-output constraints, including how fallback_suggestion must surface.
Synthesizer will merge the documented state_delta points into a final executable plan and note any remaining evidence gaps for the Planner.
risks: Unable to execute actual agents in this context, so assurance remains theoretical until runtime evidence is available.
Verification relies entirely on static documentation, so any undisclosed runtime constraints remain unknown.
No current harness to merge real state_delta outputs, meaning integration issues might surface later.
next_steps: Retriever: summarize the Research Pipeline acceptance criteria and structured-output expectations from Codex全局增强方案-候选清单.md and codex-global-multi-agent/README.md, producing evidence entries tied to those files.
Verifier: confirm that the base contract’s prompt stack, schema adherence, recovery, and fallback rules (from prompts/shared/base_contract.md) satisfy the protocol, noting any missing artifacts.

Synthesizer: combine the documented state_delta facts into a concise executable verification plan, ensure state merging logic references the shared contract, and highlight any residual evidence needs before handing off to the Planner.
